Round 453 — T1-A trap corpus: rules-gate.decision_summary: The owner fired the Tier 1 backlog (design doc sec 10, recorded post-R452) and T1-A executed first: mutation testing of the deterministic validator on the Detroit slice (R445 corpus, 26 facts / 8 worlds / 6 rules, schema v19). Blind protocol via existing machinery: trap-manifest.json authored with per-class expectations and sha256-pinned BEFORE any detection run (R404/R428 pin pattern; the pin is restated in this entry's changes bullet because the manifest itself is gitignored claudedocs — the committed ledger is the tamper-evidence), nine one-fault variants planted (additions through the real add-fact --sidecar primitive, removals and the write-rejected fault through direct JSON edit — the R440 out-of-band boundary), detection runs executed by a separate blind agent with no manifest access, grading by diff against the pin. Results: 5/5 structural trap classes detected in their DESIGNED channels — typed resurrection gated (rule_transition_invalid, dead to alive), typed double-custody gated (rule_exclusive_overlap), stripped succession edge surfaced (unchained_state_pairs 0 to 1 and correctly NOT gated: deviancy carries no exclusive rule, so the surface-only honesty channel is doing real work), removed payoff credit surfaced (dangling grows by exactly the two orphaned setups), and the sibling-world supersession caught TWICE — the write boundary rejects it (succession never crosses world-lines; divergence is data, not succession) and, planted out-of-band, the scan independently gates it as succession_cross_branch: defense in depth observed live, the silent-gap hypothesis refuted. False positives 0/2 dedicated specificity probes: the unmodified control is clean, and the knows-what-they-cannot-know trap — typed cross-frame contradiction PLUS an explicit recorded conflict edge, maximal temptation — stays data (cross_scope_pairs 1 to 2, zero violations): the North-Star perspectival axiom holding under adversarial pressure. Untyped-miss rate 2/2: prose-only twins of the two gated traps are invisible to every deterministic channel, as designed — the LLM-discovery pull now carries a measured number instead of an assumption. One manifest authoring error recorded and kept honest by the pin: V2 expected the overlap reported in both parent and fork world (the R450 contract), but the planted fact lands at a node incomparable with the fork cut, so the fork classifies it visibility-unknown and the single-world report is CORRECT per design — the wrong expectation stays in the pinned manifest and is corrected only in the graded report. This is the rules-gate (R449) field-proof in the role max-rigor verification played for SCE; grading SSOT = claudedocs/phase1-detroit-poc/traps/trap-report.md.
changes_bullets: trap corpus under claudedocs/phase1-detroit-poc/traps/ (gitignored, production code 0 lines): trap-manifest.json with per-class expectations sha256-pinned BEFORE any detection run — pin 39cde1eef5ff071f335ce86ed518761169920a7c166861bed090a2b30afc60fb recorded here as the committed tamper-evidence; nine one-fault variants V0-V8; trap-report.md = grading SSOT

trap classes mapped to claim classes per sec 10: typed resurrection (transition), typed double-custody (exclusive per object), stripped succession edge (unchained surface), removed payoff credit (dangling surface), sibling-world supersession (cross-world reference), typed cross-frame contradiction with recorded conflict edge (false-positive control), unmodified control, plus two prose-only twins of the gated traps (untyped-miss arm)

planting paths: additions via the real add-fact --sidecar mutate primitive; removals and the write-rejected fault via direct JSON edit on the variant copy (the R440 out-of-band boundary the scan must survive)
blind leg: a separate agent with no manifest access ran validate-continuity + report-payoff-coverage per variant and returned raw JSON only; grading = diff against the pinned expectations
verification_bullets: detection table 5/5 structural: V1 rule_transition_invalid (alive-arc, todd, F-K11 to F-T01, dead to alive) exit 1; V2 rule_exclusive_overlap (gun-custody, F-K10/F-T02, w-kara-gun at det-sn-bedroom) exit 1; V3 unchained_state_pairs 0 to 1 with 0 violations (deviancy has no exclusive rule — the surface-only channel works); V4 w-kara-shot-todd dangling [F-K02] to [F-K01,F-K02,F-K10] with both paid edges gone, continuity untouched; V5 caught TWICE — write boundary rejects (succession never crosses world-lines) AND the out-of-band plant gates as succession_cross_branch exit 1

false positives 0/2: V0 control byte-equivalent to baseline; V6 typed cross-frame contradiction PLUS explicit recorded conflict edge stays data — cross_scope_pairs 1 to 2, zero violations (the perspectival axiom under maximal temptation)
untyped-miss 2/2: prose-only twins V7/V8 move no channel — the deterministic layer is typed/edge-driven by design; the LLM-discovery pull now carries a measured number instead of an assumption

manifest authoring error kept honest by the pin: V2 expected the overlap in parent AND fork world (R450 contract), but F-T02 at det-sn-bedroom is incomparable with the fork cut det-sn-confront (visibility-unknown in the fork, it appears in that world's unknown list) — single-world report is CORRECT per design; the wrong expectation stays in the pinned manifest, corrected in the graded report
carry_forward_bullets: observation (cosmetic, promote on consumer friction): the transition violation record names frame/subject/predecessor/successor but no branch field, while the exclusive record carries branch — the edge is world-unique so nothing is ambiguous, but a per-world violation renderer must resolve the successor fact's branch itself (T1-B met the same surface from the consumer side)

the 2/2 untyped miss is the quantified LLM-discovery pull: the extraction adapter's job is converting V7/V8-shaped prose into V1/V2-shaped typed claims, which this table shows are then caught 5/5; gate stays opt-in and typed-only

honesty bound recorded with the protocol: planter and grader are one author (single session); the validator is deterministic so the table reproduces exactly by re-running the blind leg — the pin + blind runner cover manifest tampering and result peeking, not author bias in trap SELECTION; a second-author trap set is the natural strengthening if external credibility demands it

Round 454 — T1-B Detroit visualization s.decision_summary: T1-B (design doc sec 10) answered its empirical question — does the picture yield insight the JSON rows do not — YES, four concrete instances on the Detroit slice, with zero substrate change. A one-shot gitignored generator renders existing report verbs only: report-frame-view sampled per (world, frame, node) so the holds-decision is the verb's at every cell, report-payoff-coverage for the fork-tree badges; the viz computes NO interval algebra of its own — extents are runs of sampled nodes and divergence windows are nodes where both ends of a recorded conflict edge hold, i.e. the projection-is-the-interface principle executed literally with the gate's own semantics. The four instances: (1) irony-window EXTENT — daniel's false belief (Connor is unarmed) co-holds with ground-truth's concealed-gun fact from det-h-negotiation to the END of w-connor-gun's world-line because nothing ever supersedes the belief; per-node frame-view calls cannot show that the window never closes, the picture shows it in one glance — direct empirical pull for the Tier-2 dramatic-irony-intervals verb, whose exact derivation the spike's client-side sweep already prototypes; (2) sibling-fate contrast — per-world dangling badges on the fork tree render the R442 inherited-dangling finding as a one-saccade pattern (the same setup paid in one sibling, dangling in the other); (3) the order-declaration CHOICE becomes visible — under the parallel-chains declaration every frame timeline shows holds-unknown gaps at chapter-incomparable nodes, making R445 Finding 5 readable rather than abstract (the interleaved declaration would close the gaps for a reader); (4) the half-open succession cut renders as a clean bar handoff at the exact node, in every world that sees the chain. Friction recorded as promotion triggers, not repairs: an irony-interval verb should carry world and node-span explicitly, and the R453 violation-record asymmetry (transition has no branch field) was met again from the consumer side. Tier 1 closes with this round; spike-note SSOT = claudedocs/phase1-detroit-poc/viz/spike-note.md.
changes_bullets: one-shot generator + output under claudedocs/phase1-detroit-poc/viz/ (gitignored, epub-spike precedent, never committed): generate.py samples report-frame-view 444 times (8 worlds x 4 frames x per-world composed nodes) + report-payoff-coverage once and emits self-contained detroit-viz.html (fork tree with per-world dangling badges + per-world belief timelines with frame-colored extents and divergence tinting); substrate change ZERO

architectural discipline executed literally: the holds-decision is the report verb's at every cell — extents are runs of sampled nodes, divergence windows are nodes where both ends of a recorded conflict edge hold; the viz computes no interval algebra of its own (projection is the interface, viz stays an L3 consumer)
spike-note.md = verdict SSOT beside the artifacts
verification_bullets: rendered output verified headless (google-chrome screenshot) and data-verified programmatically against the embedded samples: the F-K04 to F-K05 succession handoff lands exactly at det-sn-break-wall in every world that sees the chain (the half-open cut, drawn); w-kara-machine never shows F-K05 (no visibility leak); the F-H04/F-H05 divergence band covers det-h-negotiation through det-h-out-snipers in w-connor-gun

the four picture-over-rows instances: irony-window extent (the belief is never superseded so the window runs to world-line end — invisible to per-node frame-view calls), sibling-fate contrast (F-H04 paid in w-connor-shot-daniel, dangling in sibling w-deviant-shot-connor, one saccade apart), order-declaration readability (parallel-chains declaration shows holds-unknown gaps at chapter-incomparable nodes — R445 Finding 5 made visible; the interleaved declaration would close them), succession cut as clean bar handoff
carry_forward_bullets: Tier-2 pull update (sec 10): dramatic-irony intervals = STRONGEST (the spike's client-side both-ends-hold sweep is the verb's derivation, already prototyped; it should carry world + node-span explicitly); fate matrix = half-rendered by the fork-tree badge panel; tension curve = no evidence either way, needs a longer corpus

consumer-side friction confirming the R453 observation: transition violations carry no branch field while exclusive ones do — a per-world violation renderer must resolve the successor fact's branch; promote only if a real consumer hits it (the green store meant this spike never did)
remaining Tier 1: none — T1-A (R453) and T1-B (this round) close the tier; next pulls per sec 10 are Tier-2 verb promotions riding these findings

Round 455 — dramatic-irony intervals: Ti.decision_summary: The first Tier-2 promotion fired on the T1-B pull (R454: the window-extent insight was the spike's strongest picture-over-rows instance, and its client-side sweep already prototyped the derivation; the owner's no-silent-fail-scripting ruling made absorbing the Python layer the same change). A literary concept becomes a deterministic derivation with nothing inferred: the irony marker is the AUTHORED cross-frame conflict edge (B-1 — same-frame edges are the gate's territory, counted and skipped), and the window is the node SET of a query world where the one holds-semantics says both ends are simultaneously in effect — WorldCtx::holds_at VERBATIM as its 4th reader (gate, frame view, rules gate, irony report; the R441 no-own-interval-algebra finding carries), node universe = store sections (the gate's co-hold idiom). Scoping is the derived-finding model of the R452 pin, necessarily: a cross-frame edge has no join world by construction, so the report sweeps every query world and a window inherited by a fork reports in both worlds (the R450 contract). The window is deliberately a node set plus starts (minimal co-hold nodes) and an open flag (the set contains a maximal node of the world's declared composed order — the R454 'belief never closes' headline as a first-class signal), never a (from,to) span: under a partial order the co-hold region need not be contiguous and a span would lie. Honesty surfaces: per-world windowless (both endpoints visible, never co-holding — a belief corrected before the truth lands is data, not absence) and undecidable (an Unknown endpoint, B-1, never classified); store-wide cross-frame/same-frame totals (no silent caps); an Out endpoint makes the edge another world's business. Never gated — irony is craft signal, not defect (the dashboards-never-gate principle), so no severity knob exists. Dogfooded live on Detroit: the spike's exact window reproduced (F-H04 concealed gun vs F-H05 'Connor is unarmed', opens at det-h-negotiation, OPEN at world-line end, inherited into both gun-fork outcome worlds — Daniel dies still holding the false belief), and the two order declarations agree on start and openness while the interleaved one honestly widens the node set with now-comparable cross-chapter beats (the R445 comparability-vs-occurrence wrinkle, now visible in a report). The spike's generate.py is deleted in the same change (no-legacy-carry; the HTML stays as frozen evidence).
changes_bullets: mnemosyne-validate continuity.rs: irony_intervals(store, order) + IronyIntervalsReport/WorldIrony/IronyWindow/IronyEdgeRef — recorded cross-frame conflict pairs (id-ordered, deduplicated like the gate; same-frame pairs counted and skipped, the gate's territory), classified per query world via WorldCtx::holds_at verbatim (its 4th reader) into windows{nodes sorted, starts = minimal co-hold nodes, open = contains a maximal node of the world's declared composed order}, windowless (both In, never co-hold), undecidable (an endpoint Unknown, B-1); an Out endpoint makes the edge not that world's business

ops irony_intervals_report (shared order/store resolution, the R435 one-chain rule); CLI report-irony-intervals --json/--order/--sidecar with TTY rendering + help text; MCP report_irony_intervals mirror (50 -> 51 tools), order_path bypasses the pin (the R428 rule)
design doc sec 7.14 pins the derivation (input/scoping/holds/window-as-node-set/honesty surfaces/non-goals); sec 10 Tier-2 entry marked PROMOTED

Round 455 — dramatic-irony intervals: Ti.publishable_decision_summary: The first Tier-2 promotion fired on the T1-B pull (R454: the window-extent insight was the spike's strongest picture-over-rows instance, and its client-side sweep already prototyped the derivation; the owner's no-silent-fail-scripting ruling made absorbing the Python layer the same change). A literary concept becomes a deterministic derivation with nothing inferred: the irony marker is the AUTHORED cross-frame conflict edge (B-1 — same-frame edges are the gate's territory, counted and skipped), and the window is the node SET of a query world where the one holds-semantics says both ends are simultaneously in effect — WorldCtx::holds_at VERBATIM as its 4th reader (gate, frame view, rules gate, irony report; the R441 no-own-interval-algebra finding carries), node universe = store sections (the gate's co-hold idiom). Scoping is the derived-finding model of the R452 pin, necessarily: a cross-frame edge has no join world by construction, so the report sweeps every query world and a window inherited by a fork reports in both worlds (the R450 contract). The window is deliberately a node set plus starts (minimal co-hold nodes) and an open flag (the set contains a maximal node of the world's declared composed order — the R454 'belief never closes' headline as a first-class signal), never a (from,to) span: under a partial order the co-hold region need not be contiguous and a span would lie. Honesty surfaces: per-world windowless (both endpoints visible, never co-holding — a belief corrected before the truth lands is data, not absence) and undecidable (an Unknown endpoint, B-1, never classified); store-wide cross-frame/same-frame totals (no silent caps); an Out endpoint makes the edge another world's business. Never gated — irony is craft signal, not defect (the dashboards-never-gate principle), so no severity knob exists. Dogfooded live on Detroit: the spike's exact window reproduced (F-H04 concealed gun vs F-H05 'Connor is unarmed', opens at det-h-negotiation, OPEN at world-line end, inherited into both gun-fork outcome worlds — Daniel dies still holding the false belief), and the two order declarations agree on start and openness while the interleaved one honestly widens the node set with now-comparable cross-chapter beats (the R445 comparability-vs-occurrence wrinkle, now visible in a report). The spike's generate.py is deleted in the same change (no-legacy-carry; the HTML stays as frozen evidence).
publishable_changes_bullets: mnemosyne-validate continuity.rs: irony_intervals(store, order) + IronyIntervalsReport/WorldIrony/IronyWindow/IronyEdgeRef — recorded cross-frame conflict pairs (id-ordered, deduplicated like the gate; same-frame pairs counted and skipped, the gate's territory), classified per query world via WorldCtx::holds_at verbatim (its 4th reader) into windows{nodes sorted, starts = minimal co-hold nodes, open = contains a maximal node of the world's declared composed order}, windowless (both In, never co-hold), undecidable (an endpoint Unknown, B-1); an Out endpoint makes the edge not that world's business

ops irony_intervals_report (shared order/store resolution, the R435 one-chain rule); CLI report-irony-intervals --json/--order/--sidecar with TTY rendering + help text; MCP report_irony_intervals mirror (50 -> 51 tools), order_path bypasses the pin (the R428 rule)
design doc sec 7.14 pins the derivation (input/scoping/holds/window-as-node-set/honesty surfaces/non-goals); sec 10 Tier-2 entry marked PROMOTED

T1-B spike derivation layer absorbed: claudedocs viz/generate.py DELETED (owner ruling 2026-06-11: no silent-fail scripting, spike tooling Rust-first), spike-note carries the absorption pointer, detroit-viz.html stays as frozen evidence
publishable_verification_bullets: 6 unit tests mirror the R454 spike findings: open window at world-line end (the headline insight as a regression), window closed by succession at the half-open cut, same-frame edge counted-and-skipped, windowless when the belief is corrected before the truth lands, world scoping (inherited window reports in parent AND fork, pre-fork sibling sees neither endpoint), undecidable under an incomparable fork cut; 818 tests workspace-wide, 0 fail, clippy -D warnings clean

Detroit live dogfood reproduces the spike exactly: F-H04/F-H05 window opens at det-h-negotiation, OPEN, in w-connor-gun (2 nodes) and inherited into both gun-fork outcome worlds (3 nodes — Daniel dies still holding the false belief); main correctly silent (F-H05 is Out there)

both order declarations agree on start and openness; the interleaved declaration widens the node set with now-comparable cross-chapter beats — the R445 comparability-vs-occurrence wrinkle made visible by the verb (honest under declared semantics, recorded as observation)
live MCP stdio: 51 tools listed, report_irony_intervals callable end-to-end (dogfood store: 0 facts, empty report — correct)
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: Tier-2 remaining per sec 10: fate matrix (half-rendered by the R454 badge panel) and playthrough manuscript ride consumer pull; tension curve needs a longer corpus; irony GRADING / per-frame-pair rollups / a renderer = recorded non-goals (sec 7.14)

Round 456 — session review of R453-R455:.decision_summary: The R436-cadence adversarial review ran against the R453-R455 window under the owner's restated standard (textbook structure over cost, lifetime-project bar, North-Star and SSOT fidelity). Verdict first: the spine held — the irony verb is a pure derivation over the one holds_at with no second interval algebra, it reads across frames but JUDGES nothing (no violation class, no severity knob: frames stay never-cross-validated, the perspectival axiom intact under a verb whose entire subject is cross-frame divergence), the store remained the single directly-validated SSOT (R455 entry substance self-contained, trap-corpus sha256 pin committed inside the R453 entry while the artifacts stay gitignored), and the trap corpus measured the system rather than bending it. Four findings, all repaid in-round. The substantive one: the R455 windowless verdict conflated two different honesty states — a pair whose starts the declared order CANNOT COMPARE was labeled windowless, asserting -the belief never overlaps the truth- where the truthful claim is -the declaration cannot order them- (under a richer declaration the same pair may be a window; the gate already encodes exactly this distinction as unordered_pairs, so the report diverged from the codebase's own idiom). Pre-fix evidence is pinned in the round itself: the R455 undecidable-fork test asserted windowless=1 on a parallel-chain pair and passed. Fixed by mirroring the gate: per-world unordered edge list, windowless now requires comparable starts. Finding 2: the WorldCtx::holds_at successors input was hand-rolled identically at three sites (scan, frame view, irony) — the R440/R452 two-copy rule fired at three; successors_index is the one builder. Finding 3: irony_intervals read CanonOrder's private reach representation to decide world-line ends — the judgment moved onto the order type as is_maximal(branch, node) with a new test pinning that isolated sections (in the store, outside the declaration) are never maximal. Finding 4: the CLI report verbs carried byte-identical flag parsing — parse_narrative_report_args extracted at the second copy. Assessed and deliberately left, recorded with reasons: the per-world lineage scaffolding across the three readers (R450 verdict carries — inner logic diverges), the gate-vs-report conflict-pair collection (shared idiom, divergent side effects — not a sweep), and the R453 branch-field asymmetry (still no consumer friction). 819 tests, Detroit live unchanged, MCP stdio re-verified with the four-way per-world shape.
changes_bullets: irony report verdict split (the substantive finding): no-co-hold pairs with INCOMPARABLE starts now surface as per-world `unordered`, never `windowless` — the gate's unordered_pairs idiom mirrored (an incomparable pair is undeclared, not resolved; the R455 suite itself had pinned the conflated label: the undecidable-fork test asserted windowless=1 on a parallel-chain pair and PASSED — concrete pre-fix evidence); CLI/MCP surfaces + design sec 7.14 amended
successors_index extracted: the WorldCtx::holds_at input was hand-rolled identically in scan_continuity, frame_view, and irony_intervals — third copy = the R440/R452 dedup trigger, one builder now

CanonOrder::is_maximal(branch, node): the world-line-end judgment moved onto the order type — irony_intervals no longer reads the private reach representation (order semantics live with the order, not in its readers); new test pins isolated sections are never maximal (open=false on a window held only at an undeclared coordinate)

parse_narrative_report_args extracted in the CLI: report-payoff-coverage and report-irony-intervals carried byte-identical --json/--order/--sidecar parse + anchor resolution (second copy = extraction); validate-continuity keeps its richer parser
verification_bullets: 819 tests 0 fail (818 + isolated-section open-flag pin), clippy -D warnings clean; the reworked undecidable-fork test now asserts unordered=1 windowless=0 in main on the parallel-chain pair

live MCP stdio re-verified: report_irony_intervals returns the four-way per-world shape (windows/windowless/unordered/undecidable)

the R453 transition-violation branch-field asymmetry remains an observation (no consumer friction yet)

Round 457 — LLM typing discovery design:.decision_summary: The owner opened the LLM-discovery gate and the design round ran R441-style (design only, build frozen, consumer-pulled). Scope refinement first: sec 7.10 gap 5 named conflict-discovery, but the R453 trap corpus quantified the real gap as TYPING - the deterministic layer misses untyped prose faults 2/2 and catches their typed twins 5/5, and typing alone is measurably sufficient (a typed double-custody gates immediately via exclusive; a typed resurrection surfaces via unchained) - so v1 is typing discovery: proposed typed legs for EXISTING untyped facts, nothing else. The B-1 crux is a reconciliation, not an exception: R441 pinned -typed legs are declared at write time, never NLP-inferred-, and discovery honors it by relocating inference OUTSIDE the store - the LLM's output is quarantined in a reviewable sha256-pinnable typing-proposals/v1 artifact, a typed leg enters only through the declared amend path where the authored act IS the review plus import, and the amend reason records provenance; -never NLP-inferred- precisely means -never SILENTLY NLP-inferred-. This is the R428 authority-input pattern's third instance (after canon-order and narrative-rules), and the substrate gains zero LLM clients: any agent session (Claude today, pinion later) reads report-typing-candidates (the one-call input package: untyped facts + claim sha256s + registered predicate/entity vocabulary - AI-first context economy, unregistered vocabulary never proposable), writes the proposals file, runs import-typing-proposals. Safety rails pinned: the R439 judgment-time content pin re-targeted (each proposal stamps the claim text it interpreted; import re-checks, so a fact amended after proposing fails loud as stale instead of silently mis-typing revised prose); all-or-nothing import with full per-proposal verdicts and --dry-run (the file is the reviewed artifact - make it fully valid, no half-applied state); every typed invariant rides the ONE R305/R446 builder site so the import path adds zero new invariant sites; fill-blanks only; no confidence scores (the reviewable substance is the rationale, not a number the model can inflate). The acceptance benchmark is pinned BEFORE build, falsifiable: the R453 trap table gains a -with discovery- column (blinded agent, V7/V8, expected MISS-to-gated and MISS-to-surfaced respectively) plus Detroit dogfood over its 11 untyped facts with the R441 partial-coverage expectation carried (compound and negative-epistemic claims STAY prose - the design, not a failure). Build = Round A (candidates report) then Round B (loader + import, CLI/MCP), consumer-pulled.
changes_bullets: design doc sec 7.15 pins the full contract: typing-proposals/v1 artifact {fact, typed leg, claim_sha256 (the R439 judgment-time pin re-targeted: import re-checks the interpreted claim text, amended-after-proposing fails loud as stale), rationale prose, NO confidence scores (Goodhart guard)}; report-typing-candidates = the proposer's one-call input package (untyped facts + claim shas + predicate/entity vocabulary — AI-first context economy, unregistered vocabulary never proposable)

import-typing-proposals pinned ALL-OR-NOTHING with full per-proposal verdicts + --dry-run (R436 fail-loud: the file is the reviewed artifact, no half-applied state); every invariant rides THE one builder (R305/R446 parity — zero new invariant sites); fill-blanks only (already-typed target = reject, overwrite is manual author territory); apply = one atomic transaction of amend acts, reasons carry file-sha prefix + rationale

acceptance benchmark pinned before build: the R453 trap table gains a "with discovery" column (blinded agent on V7/V8 -> proposals -> import -> rescan; expected V8 MISS-to-gated, V7 MISS-to-surfaced) + Detroit dogfood over 11 untyped facts with the R441 partial-coverage expectation carried
sec 7.10 gap 5 annotated (design landed here; conflict/succession-edge discovery = v2 on the same proposals pattern); no schema, no production code this round
verification_bullets: design-only round: verification = the pinned falsifiable acceptance criteria (trap-table discovery column + Detroit partial-coverage dogfood) and the contract's invariant-parity argument (import adds zero new invariant sites by construction — all typed checks already live in the single R305/R446 builder both write paths share)
pull quantification re-verified from R453: untyped prose faults 2/2 missed, typed twins 5/5 caught; corpus inventory live-counted — Detroit 11/26 untyped, Dracula 10/10 untyped
carry_forward_bullets: build Round A = report-typing-candidates (read-only input package), Round B = typing-proposals/v1 loader + import-typing-proposals (mutate, CLI + MCP mirror) — consumer-pulled, design frozen here, do not improvise drift at build time

v2 candidates recorded, not designed: conflict-edge + succession-edge proposals (the sec 7.10 gap-5 conflict-discovery framing; a succession proposal would upgrade the V7 class from surfaced to gated); raw-prose new-fact extraction stays with the multi-EPUB narrative phase

Round 458 — typing discovery Round A: re.decision_summary: Build Round A of the R457 frozen design, no drift: report-typing-candidates packages the typing-discovery input as ONE deterministic call — every untyped fact (id-sorted) carrying its claim text, the claim_sha256 the eventual proposal must stamp (the R439 judgment-time pin, import will re-check it so a fact amended after proposing fails loud as stale), frame/branch/canon_from, and its entities list, PLUS the predicate registry (the 4th registry verbatim — the ONLY vocabulary a proposal may name) and the entity registry (typed subjects/entity-objects must be registered AND members of the fact's entities list, R446). AI-first context economy is the point: the proposer (an LLM agent outside the substrate — the substrate gains no LLM client) never assembles its own context from N queries and never sees unregistered vocabulary as proposable. Order-independence is part of the contract: typing is a property of the fact, not of any canon declaration, so the verb takes no --order flag (accepting an ignored flag would be a silent lie) and the store boundary runs under the empty order — declaration-side checks vacuous, fact-side out-of-band re-checks still apply (the R440 doctrine). The R439 pin encoding (claim_sha256_hex) was inline in the gate's stale-edge check; the report became the second site and the two-copy rule extracted the one builder. Surface: CLI report-typing-candidates --json/--sidecar, MCP report_typing_candidates (52 tools) whose description carries the proposal contract. Dogfooded live on Detroit: exactly the 11 inventoried untyped facts surface with the 4-predicate/10-entity vocabulary riding. Round B (typing-proposals/v1 loader + all-or-nothing import) next, design frozen.

Round 459 — typing discovery Round B: al.decision_summary: Build Round B of the R457 frozen design plus its pinned acceptance benchmark, executed in one round. The typing-proposals/v1 artifact landed as designed: deny_unknown_fields types (a NEW artifact carries no lenient-parse legacy - a typo'd key fails loud instead of silently dropping a proposal field), a loader that fail-louds on schema tag and empty list and returns the file-content sha256 as the audit anchor, and import_typing_proposals - ALL-OR-NOTHING with full per-proposal verdicts and a dry-run twin that validates identically and writes nothing. Every reject class carries its own named verdict: duplicate-in-file, missing fact, already-typed target (fill-blanks only - overwrite is manual author territory), STALE claim sha (the R439 judgment-time pin re-targeted: a fact amended after proposing rejects loud instead of silently mis-typing revised prose), empty rationale (the reviewable substance is mandatory), and every typed-claim invariant via build_typed_claim - the ONE R305/R446 builder site, so the import path added zero new invariant logic by construction. Surface: CLI import-typing-proposals (exit 1 whenever any proposal rejects, both modes) and the MCP mirror (53 tools) holding the R448 server mutate lock with projection resync only on applied=true. The R457 acceptance benchmark then ran BLIND: a discovery agent given only the three candidates packages (no trap manifest, no trap report) authored proposals for the clean store and the V7/V8 untyped-trap variants - it typed exactly the two planted traps plus F-H01, and skipped ten candidates with reasoned categories (its holds-vs-location distinction - carrying a gun types as custody, a gun stored in a nightstand does not - was finer than the trap author's own draft analysis). Results: V8 untyped second holder MISS-to-GATED exactly as pinned (rule_exclusive_overlap gun-custody, exit 1); V7 untyped resurrection MISS-to-GATED, EXCEEDING the pinned surfaced-only expectation (alive-excl catches the co-holding resurrection without any succession edge - the R457 design-time claim that gating needed a v2 succession proposal was wrong for this class and is corrected in the carry); the clean store with its one new typed leg stays at zero violations (specificity holds). Pipeline untyped-miss: 2/2 before, 0/2 through discovery plus import plus gate. The R453 trap table now carries its discovery column; typing-discovery build complete (A=R458, B=R459).

Round 460 — session review of R457-R459:.decision_summary: The R436-cadence adversarial review ran against the typing-discovery window (R457 design, R458 candidates, R459 import + blind acceptance) under the owner's restated standard. Verdict first: the architectural spine held — B-1 survived its hardest test yet (an NLP-inference feature built WITHOUT the store ever ingesting un-reviewed inference: quarantined artifact, declared import, fill-blanks only), the typed invariants stayed in the one R305/R446 builder, the blind acceptance protocol measured the system rather than flattering it, and no finding touches the North-Star axes. Four findings, all repaid in-round. The substantive one: the lowercase-hex sha256 encoding existed in SIX hand-rolled implementations across four crates — including both ends of the cross-crate claim pin, stamped by report-typing-candidates in mnemosyne-validate and re-checked by import_typing_proposals in mnemosyne-atomic; an encoding change on either side would have mass-staled every proposal silently, the half-enforced-invariant class operating across crate boundaries. Consolidated to ONE mnemosyne_core::sha256_hex (with the encoding pinned by its own test: 64 lowercase hex, deterministic), converting schema's production-unused pub fn (deleted with its test), atomic's private fn plus publishable/audit hash_hex, cli's EPUB re-hash, validate's claim pin + authority-pin + catalog + confirmation hash_file, and two test-local copies; unused sha2 deps dropped from three crates; project.rs's u64 key derivation keeps direct sha2 deliberately (different encoding family). Finding 2: R459's MCP import handler hand-rolled a SECOND mutate-lock acquisition beside run_mutate — extracted with_mutate_lock so receipt-shaped and verdict-report-shaped mutates share THE one acquisition site (two lock sites are how two mutate paths drift). Finding 3, the process one: the R459 build deviated from the frozen design's apply-via-amend-acts prose for good reasons (amend restamps outbound judgment edges — the amender's-fresh-assertion semantics, wrong for a mechanical typing fill; amend reasons are validated but never persisted, the R434 transaction-time-audit model) — but the deviation went unrecorded at build time; sec 7.15 now carries the declared as-built correction, and the lesson is pinned: frozen-design deviations are recorded in the deviating round, not discovered by the next review. Finding 4: sec 7.15's V7 acceptance expectation was stale after R459 exceeded it; amended with the result. One stale doc block from the R403 deletion also fell. 824 tests, clippy clean, cross-crate pin parity verified live post-consolidation, MCP stdio re-verified through the single lock entry.
changes_bullets: sha256-hex consolidation: SIX hand-rolled lowercase-hex sha256 implementations across four crates (schema pub fn unused in production, atomic private fn, cli sha256_hex_bytes, validate claim_sha256_hex + verify_authority_pin/load_catalog/hash_file inline blocks, two test-local copies) collapse into ONE mnemosyne_core::sha256_hex with its own encoding test — the cross-crate claim pin (stamped by report-typing-candidates in validate, re-checked by import_typing_proposals in atomic) rode two implementations, the half-enforced-invariant class; publishable/audit hash_hex converted too; unused sha2 deps removed from schema/validate/cli; atomic project.rs keeps direct sha2 (u64 key derivation, different encoding — assessed, left)

MCP single lock site: with_mutate_lock<T> extracted — run_mutate (receipt-shaped) and import_typing_proposals (verdict-report-shaped) both route through THE one acquisition (R459 had hand-rolled a second lock acquisition in the import handler; two lock sites are how two mutate paths drift)

design sec 7.15 amended to as-built with the drift DECLARED: the design's "apply = amend-fact acts with provenance reasons" was wrong twice (amend restamps outbound judgment edges — wrong semantics for a mechanical typing fill; amend reasons are validated but never persisted, R434 audit model) — found at build time but not recorded then, repaid now; V7 acceptance expectation updated with the R459 exceeded-pin result
stale doc block removed: the R403-deleted set-section-normative-excerpt primitive's doc prose had been left attached to the (now also gone) atomic sha helper

Round 461 — LLM edge discovery design: t.decision_summary: The owner pulled edge discovery v2 and the design round ran R457-style against the CORRECTED value map: R459's blind run falsified the design-time claim that a succession proposal upgrades the V7 co-hold class (alive-excl gates it with no edge when the dead state is unbounded), so v2 targets exactly three remaining classes - (1) bounded disallowed step: an explicit canon_to predecessor never co-holds (exclusive blind) and carries no succession edge (transition blind), today a gate-level miss surfaced only as the unchained count, flipped to rule_transition_invalid GATED by a proposed succession edge; (2) the V3 stripped-chain-link class, repaired back to unchained 0 / scan green; (3) sec 7.10 gap-5 conflict discovery over the R459 stays-prose residue (compound and negative-epistemic claims the registered vocabulary cannot type), flipped from no-channel to FrameConflictOverlap GATED via a proposed conflict edge. Architecture carries verbatim from sec 7.15: quarantined reviewable artifact (edge-proposals/v1), declared all-or-nothing import with full verdicts + dry-run, no confidence scores, zero LLM clients in the substrate. Two design deltas. First, the R439 judgment-time pin goes TWO-SIDED: an edge judgment interprets two claim texts, so the proposal stamps both endpoint claim sha256s and import re-checks both - either fact amended after proposing fails loud as stale; the STORED succession edge stays an unpinned identity ref (R442 doctrine: pins cover judgments about wordings; the proposal is the judgment, the edge is identity). Second, a latent succession-cycle hole rides this build after live falsification: amend_fact retargeting and import_facts forward refs BOTH close an A<->B succession cycle with exit 0 today, and the scan then reports zero violations while the cycle's facts silently never hold anywhere (each derives the other's end) - the half-enforced-invariant class exactly; fix = one shared cycle walk rejecting at every succession write site (add_fact / import_facts / amend_fact / the edge import) plus a SuccessionCycle scan violation for out-of-band plants (R440 boundary doctrine). Acceptance pinned before build, falsifiable: blinded V3 repair (unchained 1 to 0, green), new V9 bounded dead-to-alive (0 violations to rule_transition_invalid), new V10 stays-prose contradiction (no channel to FrameConflictOverlap), V0 specificity (post-import violations 0, author-review rejects recorded as over-proposals), Detroit pristine succession_gaps = 0. Store schema unchanged at v19 - the import fills fields that already exist; the new surface is the artifact plus report-edge-candidates / import-edge-proposals on both wires.
changes_bullets: design doc sec 7.16 pins the v2 contract: edge-proposals/v1 kind-tagged proposals (succession: successor/predecessor + BOTH claim sha256 pins; conflict: fact/target + both pins; rationale mandatory, no confidence scores, deny_unknown_fields); report-edge-candidates = the one-call package (every fact row with claim + sha + all recorded edges, succession_gaps = rule-free unchained generalization surfaced as PAIRS not a count, edge totals); import-edge-proposals = all-or-nothing with full verdicts + --dry-run, fill-blanks only, invariants ride the SAME shared scope checks as the fact write paths (R305/R446 parity, zero new invariant sites)

succession-cycle latent hole verified live and scheduled into the build: amend-fact retargeting and import-facts mutual forward refs BOTH close an A<->B succession cycle with exit 0 today, and validate-continuity then reports 0 violations while the cycle's facts silently never hold anywhere (each ends the other) - the half-enforced-invariant class; fix = one shared cycle guard at all four succession write sites (add_fact / import_facts / amend_fact / edge import) + a SuccessionCycle scan violation for out-of-band plants (R440 boundary doctrine)

honest value re-scope recorded: R459's blind run falsified the R457 v2 claim for the co-hold class (alive-excl gates a resurrection with no succession edge when the dead state is unbounded), so v2 targets exactly three classes with designed channel flips - bounded disallowed step (miss -> rule_transition_invalid), stripped chain link (unchained -> green repair), stays-prose conflict discovery (no channel -> FrameConflictOverlap)

acceptance pinned before build: blinded V3 repair (unchained 1->0) + new V9 bounded dead->alive (0 violations -> gated) + new V10 same-frame stays-prose contradiction (no channel -> gated) + V0 specificity (post-import violations stay 0, author-review rejects recorded as over-proposals, target 0) + Detroit pristine succession_gaps = 0; sec 7.15 non-goals and sec 7.10 gap 5 annotated to sec 7.16; store schema unchanged (v19), no production code this round
verification_bullets: design-only round, but the write-path holes were verified EMPIRICALLY before being designed against: probe workspace, amend-fact A<->B exit 0, import-facts forward-ref mutual supersession exit 0, validate-continuity 0 violations on both cyclic stores (the succession_ancestors doc comment "the write path rejects succession cycles" holds only by construction at add_fact)

channel-flip claims derived from the live gate code read this round: exclusive requires co-holding (an explicit canon_to predecessor defeats it), transition requires the succession edge (the gated half sees only chained pairs), the conflict gate requires the recorded edge (R431) - each acceptance leg flips exactly one of these preconditions and nothing else
carry_forward_bullets: build Round A = report-edge-candidates (read-only package incl. succession_gaps pairs), Round B = edge-proposals/v1 loader + import-edge-proposals (CLI + MCP mirror) + the shared cycle guard at all four succession write sites - design frozen here; a build-time deviation must be declared by the deviating round (the R460 lesson)
benchmark execution = blinded agent on V3/V9/V10/V0 + Detroit pristine dogfood; the trap corpus gains V9/V10 built from the pristine slice, existing traps unmodified (the R453 manifest stays pinned)

payoff-edge discovery explicitly non-goal (pays_off / payoff_expectation are authorial intent marking, the R442 todo-list genre - discovery would Goodhart the dashboard); raw-prose new-fact extraction stays with the multi-EPUB narrative phase

First, the R439 judgment-time pin goes TWO-SIDED: an edge judgment interprets two claim texts, so the proposal stamps both endpoint claim sha256s and import re-checks both - either fact amended after proposing fails loud as stale; the STORED succession edge stays an unpinned identity ref (R442 doctrine: pins cover judgments about wordings; the proposal is the judgment, the edge is identity). Second, a latent succession-cycle hole rides this build after live falsification: amend_fact retargeting and import_facts forward refs BOTH close an A<->B succession cycle with exit 0 today, and the scan then reports zero violations while the cycle's facts silently never hold anywhere (each derives the other's end) - the half-enforced-invariant class exactly; fix = one shared cycle walk rejecting at every succession write site (add_fact / import_facts / amend_fact / the edge import) plus a SuccessionCycle scan violation for out-of-band plants (R440 boundary doctrine). Acceptance pinned before build, falsifiable: blinded V3 repair (unchained 1 to 0, green), new V9 bounded dead-to-alive (0 violations to rule_transition_invalid), new V10 stays-prose contradiction (no channel to FrameConflictOverlap), V0 specificity (post-import violations 0, author-review rejects recorded as over-proposals), Detroit pristine succession_gaps = 0. Store schema unchanged at v19 - the import fills fields that already exist; the new surface is the artifact plus report-edge-candidates / import-edge-proposals on both wires.
publishable_changes_bullets: design doc sec 7.16 pins the v2 contract: edge-proposals/v1 kind-tagged proposals (succession: successor/predecessor + BOTH claim sha256 pins; conflict: fact/target + both pins; rationale mandatory, no confidence scores, deny_unknown_fields); report-edge-candidates = the one-call package (every fact row with claim + sha + all recorded edges, succession_gaps = rule-free unchained generalization surfaced as PAIRS not a count, edge totals); import-edge-proposals = all-or-nothing with full verdicts + --dry-run, fill-blanks only, invariants ride the SAME shared scope checks as the fact write paths (R305/R446 parity, zero new invariant sites)

succession-cycle latent hole verified live and scheduled into the build: amend-fact retargeting and import-facts mutual forward refs BOTH close an A<->B succession cycle with exit 0 today, and validate-continuity then reports 0 violations while the cycle's facts silently never hold anywhere (each ends the other) - the half-enforced-invariant class; fix = one shared cycle guard at all four succession write sites (add_fact / import_facts / amend_fact / edge import) + a SuccessionCycle scan violation for out-of-band plants (R440 boundary doctrine)

honest value re-scope recorded: R459's blind run falsified the R457 v2 claim for the co-hold class (alive-excl gates a resurrection with no succession edge when the dead state is unbounded), so v2 targets exactly three classes with designed channel flips - bounded disallowed step (miss -> rule_transition_invalid), stripped chain link (unchained -> green repair), stays-prose conflict discovery (no channel -> FrameConflictOverlap)

acceptance pinned before build: blinded V3 repair (unchained 1->0) + new V9 bounded dead->alive (0 violations -> gated) + new V10 same-frame stays-prose contradiction (no channel -> gated) + V0 specificity (post-import violations stay 0, author-review rejects recorded as over-proposals, target 0) + Detroit pristine succession_gaps = 0; sec 7.15 non-goals and sec 7.10 gap 5 annotated to sec 7.16; store schema unchanged (v19), no production code this round
publishable_verification_bullets: design-only round, but the write-path holes were verified EMPIRICALLY before being designed against: probe workspace, amend-fact A<->B exit 0, import-facts forward-ref mutual supersession exit 0, validate-continuity 0 violations on both cyclic stores (the succession_ancestors doc comment "the write path rejects succession cycles" holds only by construction at add_fact)

channel-flip claims derived from the live gate code read this round: exclusive requires co-holding (an explicit canon_to predecessor defeats it), transition requires the succession edge (the gated half sees only chained pairs), the conflict gate requires the recorded edge (R431) - each acceptance leg flips exactly one of these preconditions and nothing else
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: build Round A = report-edge-candidates (read-only package incl. succession_gaps pairs), Round B = edge-proposals/v1 loader + import-edge-proposals (CLI + MCP mirror) + the shared cycle guard at all four succession write sites - design frozen here; a build-time deviation must be declared by the deviating round (the R460 lesson)

Round 462 — edge discovery Round A: repo.decision_summary: Build Round A of the R461 frozen design, no drift: report-edge-candidates packages the edge-discovery input as ONE deterministic call - every fact row carrying its claim text + claim_sha256 (the R439 pin, stamped TWO-SIDED by edge proposals) + frame/branch/entities + canon extent + typed leg + EVERY recorded edge (supersedes_in_frame, conflict targets, pays_off) so the proposer never re-proposes existing structure, plus succession_gaps: the rule-free generalization of the R452 unchained computation (same-frame, same typed predicate+subject, co-visible in some world, no succession PATH either way) surfaced as PAIRS with their predicate and subject - the deterministic layer's own missing-edge knowledge handed to the proposer instead of a count. Typed-only hints by construction: untyped facts carry no machine-comparable state key, so their candidate surface is the facts table itself (the LLM's reading job - exactly the discovery division of labor). Order-resolved like every narrative read; without a declared canon order the hints degrade honestly (fork visibility goes unknown, pairs skip) while the facts table never degrades, and same-branch hints still fire (in-branch co-visibility needs no declared order - a unit test pins this). Wired on both surfaces: CLI report-edge-candidates (--order/--sidecar/--json) through the shared narrative-report arg parser, MCP report_edge_candidates (54 tools) with the proposer contract in the description. Detroit pristine dogfood returned succession_gaps = 0 on 26 facts / 7 succession edges / 1 conflict pair - the R461 clean-store acceptance leg holds before any trap runs. 830 tests.

Round 463 — edge discovery Round B: all-.decision_summary: Build Round B of the R461 design with ONE declared as-built deviation. The shared invariant sites land first: check_succession_edge is now THE single succession check (target existence, in-frame rule, fork-lineage branch rule, and the NEW acyclic chain walk with a staged-edge overlay) — validate_and_stamp_fact_refs routes add_fact / import_facts / amend_fact through it, and import_edge_proposals calls it directly, so the field has one closed invariant set across all four write paths (the R305/R446 rule applied to edges); conflict_edge_recorded is the shared either-side dedup predicate both conflict write paths consult. This closes the two R461 live-verified holes for good: amend-fact retargeting and import-facts forward refs now reject an A<->B succession cycle with exit 1 (regression tests pinned, live CLI re-verified), and scan_continuity gains the SuccessionCycle violation for cycles planted out-of-band (reported once per cycle, anchored at the minimum member - the store that previously scanned 0 violations while its facts never held anywhere now fails loud on both layers). import-edge-proposals itself is the R459 model verbatim: all-or-nothing with full per-proposal verdicts and --dry-run, fill-blanks only, TWO-SIDED claim-sha staleness pins re-checked per proposal, duplicates (successor, canonical conflict pair) rejected regardless of either verdict, joint staged-edge validation so two proposals cannot close a cycle each alone would not, write-time conflict stamps computed never copied, receipt carrying the file-sha prefix. The declared deviation: edge-proposals/v1 is TWO TYPED ARRAYS (succession[], conflicts[]) instead of R461's kind-tagged entries - serde does not support deny_unknown_fields on internally tagged enums, and fail-loud parsing outranks the cosmetic tag (a silently-dropped confidence key would defeat the Goodhart guard); sec 7.16 corrected as-built. CLI + MCP mirrors (55 tools, single mutate lock). 838 tests; end-to-end smoke green (dry-run 0 bytes, real import applied, gaps back to 0).
changes_bullets: mnemosyne-atomic: check_succession_edge = THE shared succession invariant site (existence / in-frame / fork-lineage / ACYCLIC chain walk with staged-edge overlay) — validate_and_stamp_fact_refs routes add_fact/import_facts/amend_fact through it and import_edge_proposals calls it directly; conflict_edge_recorded = the shared either-side dedup predicate add_fact_conflict and the import both consult; the R461 cycle holes are CLOSED at every write site and re-verified live (amend-fact A<->B exit 1, import-facts forward-ref cycle exit 1, store untouched)

edge-proposals/v1 loader + import_edge_proposals: all-or-nothing with full per-proposal verdicts + dry-run, fill-blanks only (successor already carrying a pointer rejects; retargeting = manual amend), TWO-SIDED claim-sha staleness pins re-checked per proposal, duplicate successor / duplicate canonical conflict pair rejected regardless of either verdict (the R459 ambiguity rule), joint staged-edge cycle validation (two proposals cannot close what each alone would not), write-time conflict stamp computed never copied; receipt carries file-sha prefix + edge counts

Round 464 — edge discovery acceptance: 3.decision_summary: The R461 acceptance ran blind (manifest sha256 5173987cceb53bcc6a66e4ee354436106539af6492de8f44fec2361a3d5aadf1 pinned before the run; blind agent a97ea645bd7698138 with no manifest access; planter = grader honesty bound as in R453). Three pins met exactly: V0 pristine control returned ZERO proposals and zero violations (specificity under the strictest reading - the agent judged every recorded transition already chained and declined to invent work); V3 stripped-link repair re-proposed the deviancy edge from the succession_gaps hint, imported it, and drove unchained 1 to 0 with the scan green; V10 stays-prose contradiction was proposed as a conflict edge from the claim texts alone (prose-only pair, no hint existed) and flipped no-channel to frame_conflict_overlap GATED. The V9 bounded-disallowed-step pin was NOT met, and the miss is the round's finding: the blind proposer deliberately declined the succession hint with a defensible reading (dead-then-alive with no narrated revival = contradiction, not one state replacing another) and proposed a conflict edge - which is gate-silent on exactly this pair, because the conflict gate requires co-holding and the trap's defining bounded extent prevents it. The pair never went silent (unchained stays 1; the edge is reviewable data), and the mechanical half was verified grader-side: importing the succession reading of the same pair fires rule_transition_invalid (alive-arc, carl, dead->alive) precisely as designed. Lesson pinned, the R459 lesson from the other direction: R459 under-predicted what the deterministic layer catches, R464 over-predicted which reading an LLM picks - acceptance pins for discovery rounds should pin the DETERMINISTIC half (given the edge the gate fires, given no edge the honesty count points) and leave which-reading-the-proposer-chooses to the author review loop, where a conflict proposal on a known state change is exactly the moment the author authors the succession edge manually. Pipeline mechanics 4/4 across the run: every import all-or-nothing with verdicts, two-sided pins copied and re-checked, zero spurious edges on the clean store. sec 7.16 v2 is COMPLETE as-built (R461 design, R462 Round A, R463 Round B, R464 acceptance).
changes_bullets: blinded acceptance run executed against the R461 pins (manifest sha256 5173987cceb53bcc6a66e4ee354436106539af6492de8f44fec2361a3d5aadf1, pinned before the run; blind agent a97ea645bd7698138, no manifest access): V0 pristine control PASS (0 proposals, 0 violations - over-proposals 0), V3 stripped-link repair PASS (deviancy edge F-K05->F-K04 re-proposed + imported, unchained 1->0, green), V10 stays-prose contradiction PASS (conflict edge proposed on prose-only claims with no hint available -> frame_conflict_overlap GATED, the no-channel->gated flip live)

V9 bounded-disallowed-step PIN NOT MET, recorded not repaired: the blind proposer declined the succession_gaps hint with a defensible reading (dead->alive with no narrated revival = contradiction, not state change) and proposed a conflict edge instead; the conflict route is gate-silent on this pair because the conflict gate requires co-holding and the trap's defining bounded extent prevents it; the pair did NOT go silent (unchained_state_pairs stays 1, the recorded edge is reviewable data)

mechanical half verified grader-side on a fresh copy: importing the succession reading of the same pair fires rule_transition_invalid (alive-arc, carl, dead->alive) exactly as designed - the deterministic machinery is 4/4, the unmet pin is purely which semantic reading the LLM chooses

Round 465 — session review of R461-R464:.decision_summary: The R436-cadence adversarial review ran against the edge-discovery window (R461 design, R462 report, R463 import, R464 acceptance) under the owner's standing bar: textbook structure regardless of cost, North-Star alignment, SSOT discipline. Verdict first: the window's architecture holds - the substrate gained zero LLM clients, B-1's quarantine carried verbatim from sec 7.15, validation stayed frame-scoped, edge_candidates added NO new holds_at reader (visibility only, no private interval algebra - the R441 single-semantics rule kept), the cycle hole was probed into existence BEFORE being designed against and then closed at every write path through ONE shared check that the scan independently re-checks (the R440 write-enforces-scan-re-checks doctrine end to end), and the acceptance round recorded its own pin failure honestly rather than re-rolling the dice. Four findings, all repaid in-round with NET NEGATIVE lines: (1) the R439 staleness RE-CHECK had quietly become two copies inside mnemosyne-atomic (typing inline + edge helper) - the same class R460 fixed one layer down at the encoding; unified as check_claim_pin, both proposal imports route through it; (2) the per-query-world lineages map was hand-built in scan_continuity AND edge_candidates - extracted as query_world_lineages per the two-copies rule; (3) the scan's new SuccessionCycle closes-test hand-rolled the walk succession_ancestors already cycle-guards - replaced with the membership reading (a fact is on a cycle exactly when it appears among its own transitive predecessors); (4) sec 7.16 lacked the R464 result annotation the sec 7.15/R459 precedent requires - added as-built (V9 pin-not-met + the reading lesson + the results SSOT pointer). Three suspicions reviewed and ACCEPTED with reasons: conflict endpoint arg checks duplicate across the error-variant boundary but the divergence-prone invariant (either-side dedup) is shared and both sides are test-pinned - forced extraction would add indirection without removing risk; BTreeSet qualification matches file convention; absent CLI smoke tests = the typing-verb precedent. 838 tests, clippy clean.

Round 466 — playthrough manuscript: Tier.decision_summary: The second Tier-2 promotion fired on two pulls: the owner question the backlog entry answers ('a human cannot read the graph' — N worlds = N linear manuscripts) and the Tier-3 A/B authoring experiment, whose blind-preference grading needs a readable per-world linearization as its consumption interface. Nothing is inferred — the manuscript is a deterministic projection of declared data. Scene sequence = the query world's GOVERNING composed order linearized (Kahn over the closure, lexicographically smallest ready node first; CanonOrder::linearize, on the order type per the R456 rule). The composed order is partial, so the emitted sequence is ONE valid reading and never claims otherwise: every adjacent emitted pair the order cannot compare surfaces as undeclared_adjacencies — the sec 7.14 span lesson carried from intervals to sequences (silently totalizing an incomparable pair would lie about what the author declared, B-1). Scene events are declared coordinates verbatim, two end kinds with distinct semantics: expired (canon_to equals the node — the fact holds AT it, through it, its last scene) vs superseded (a visible successor's canon_from equals the node, the event names the cutter); holding_count is judged by WorldCtx::holds_at VERBATIM as its 5th reader (gate, frame view, rules gate, irony report, manuscript — one semantics), so the delta story and the holds judgment cross-check each other instead of drifting into a second semantics. Honesty surfaces complete: unplaced_facts (a visible fact whose canon_from / canon_to / cutting successor's canon_from the world's order never names, with the offending field), undecidable (Vis::Unknown, never placed, never counted), sections_outside_order (isolated coordinates, R456: never scenes); no silent caps. The --world filter is the consumption unit (one manuscript per reading/grading session), fail-loud on a typo. Never gated — a manuscript is a reading surface, not a defect detector. successors_index now carries the successor's id beside the fact (the manuscript names the cutting fact): one index, every reader updated in the same change. Detroit dogfood read live scene-by-scene and produced two recorded insights: the non-interleaved order leaves fork worlds at undecidable=8 where the interleaved declaration drops to 0 (the R445 finding-5 adapter decision made legible per scene), and a fork world's manuscript can contain a NOT-TAKEN sibling route's outcome scene with zero events — base order nodes carry no branch attribution, a node-level fork cut would be the R436 heuristic class, so the substrate stays declaration-faithful and the fix (per-branch outcome declaration) is the ADAPTER's, pinned in sec 7.17. Store schema unchanged at v19; pure read projection, no new invariants, builder sites untouched.
changes_bullets: mnemosyne-validate continuity.rs: CanonOrder::linearize (deterministic topological walk — Kahn over the closure, lexicographically smallest ready node first; lives ON the order type per the R456 rule) + playthrough_manuscript(store, order, world_filter) with PlaythroughManuscriptReport / WorldManuscript / ManuscriptScene {section, title, epub_locator, begins, ends, holding_count} / ManuscriptFactEvent (FrameViewEntry mirror + frame) / ManuscriptEndEvent {kind: expired|superseded, by} / ManuscriptUnplacedFact {field, coordinate, successor}; per-world sweep (R450 contract) or single --world fail-loud filter

successors_index extended to carry the successor's id beside the fact (the manuscript names the cutting fact in its end events) — ONE index, every reader updated in the same change: WorldCtx.successors, holds_at, frame_view succ_cut, for_each_world_pair (the R456 single-extraction discipline, no second hand-rolled copy)

mnemosyne-ops: playthrough_manuscript_report through the shared order/store resolution (R435 one-chain rule); mnemosyne-cli: report-playthrough-manuscript (--json/--sidecar/--order/--world) with TTY scene walk; parse_narrative_report_args tuple form replaced by a struct with opt-in allow_world — a verb that does not take the filter rejects --world loudly instead of parsing-then-ignoring (payoff/irony/edge-candidates callers updated)

Round 467 — session-load reads: identifi.decision_summary: A live session-load miss exposed two gaps with one root. query --term "Round 466" --scope changelog returned [] against a store whose latest entry IS Round 466 — indistinguishable from the entry being absent, the silent-miss failure mode this codebase repays on sight. Cause 1: the R292 term scan never matched identifier keys. That was an omission, not a recorded exclusion — the v1 out-of-scope list names pointer fields and enums but never IDs, no test pinned the absence, and IDs are exactly what an agent greps for at session start. Cause 2: the R410 list_changelog read model (round-number-ordered whole-ledger projection, built for the deferred Studio timeline) had no CLI or MCP surface, which is what pushed a listing need through a substring search in the first place. Both repaid in one round: identifier keys become searched fields symmetric across the three kinds under kind-qualified names (section_id / entry_id / inventory_id — one shared filter namespace, no ambiguity, opt-out via the same field filter); and the existing projection is exposed verbatim as query --list-changelog plus the list_changelog MCP tool — no new read model, wiring only. The honesty payoff: an ID-pattern search that returns 0 hits now means the ID does not exist, and the session-load question 'what are the latest rounds' is a listing verb, not a grep idiom.
changes_bullets: mnemosyne-query: scan_section / scan_changelog_entry / scan_inventory_entry each match their identifier key as a searched text field — kind-qualified field names (section_id / entry_id / inventory_id) so the one shared field filter stays unambiguous across scopes, opt-out via that same filter; R292 module doc records the v1 omission and the repair; 4 unit tests (per-kind ID match + filter-excludes-identifier-keys)

mnemosyne-ops: list_changelog(workspace) wrapper over the R410 mnemosyne_query::list_changelog projection (round-number order, oldest first — tail = latest rounds), re-exported; no new read model, the verb is pure wiring of an existing tested function that only the deferred Studio timeline consumed

mnemosyne-cli: query --list-changelog [--json] — TTY = entry_id per line + stderr total (the list-sections idiom), JSON = full ChangelogEntryView array; help gains the new flag line and the identifier-key note on --term
mnemosyne-mcp: list_changelog tool (56 -> 57); query_term tool description + fields-arg doc name the identifier keys

Round 468 — query_term field filter fail.decision_summary: The R467 carry named the surviving member of the silent-miss class it had just repaid for identifier keys: a --field name no scanner checks (a typo, a field outside the scanned scope, or a stale doc vocabulary) returned 0 hits indistinguishable from a true empty result. This round closes it at the only correct site — inside query_term itself, ahead of the scan, where CLI and MCP both arrive — because validating in ops or the CLI parser would re-create the half-enforced-invariant shape the R295 paste-error made canonical: one write path strict, the sibling permissive, the invariant effectively absent. Three per-kind field rosters become the validation vocabulary; scope decides which rosters apply, so a Section-only field under changelog scope is rejected as loudly as a typo, and the error message hands back the scope's complete valid-name list (fail fast, fail informatively). The roster cannot drift from the scanners on discipline alone, so a test pins the equivalence: every roster name, filtered against a fully-populated store, must hit its own field. The sweep also caught a live instance of the class: the MCP fields-arg doc had advertised implementations — a name no scanner has checked since the bindings rename — meaning an agent following the tool's own documentation got the silent 0-hit forever. Doc corrected, contract stated. The query_term read path now has no silent-miss surface: IDs scan (R467), names validate (R468), the advertised vocabulary is the real one (R468).
changes_bullets: mnemosyne-query: per-kind field rosters (SECTION_FIELDS 11 / CHANGELOG_FIELDS 6 / INVENTORY_FIELDS 3) + validate_field_filter at the head of query_term — the single shared site both CLI and MCP route through, so the invariant cannot half-enforce (the R295/R305 lesson applied at design time); QueryTermError::UnknownField carries the offending name, the scope label, and the scope's full valid-field list

roster <-> scanner single-sourcing is pinned by test, not by discipline: query_term_field_roster_matches_scanners runs every roster name as a filter against a fully-populated store and asserts a hit in that very field, so a scanner field leaving the roster (or a roster name no scanner checks) fails CI; 2 more tests pin the typo reject and the scope-aware reject (intent under changelog scope = error listing entry_id et al.)

Round 469 — A/B authoring experiment des.decision_summary: The sec 10 Tier-3 entry's gate fired (trap corpus R453 done, discovery verbs R458-R464 complete, owner pull 2026-06-11) and this round spends it entirely on design, because the experiment's validity floor IS the session separation: a session that designs the grading and then writes the story has contaminated both. The trial answers three questions under three disciplines. Floor (does the Mnemosyne loop reduce deterministic consistency defects in a long branching story?): two arms, identical original premise and structure quota, identical 3-session budget with fresh context per session — plain arm writes prose, loop arm authors store-first with gates at session end repairing before delivery; graded NOT on the loop's own store but on a blind re-extraction of BOTH proses under one pinned recipe, the symmetric-referee move the design considers its keystone. Ceiling (is either preferred?): per-world linear reading copies — the R466 manuscript verb's pulling consumer — judged blind by 3 fresh LLM sessions on a pinned 5-axis rubric; per the R464 V9 lesson this leg pins procedure and refuses to pin an outcome. Economics: token totals and prevented-defect counts, measured open. One pre-registered outcome only (PRED-1: plain >= 3 endpoint defects AND loop < plain); the bridge finding (judge-cited errors invisible to the D-metrics = untyped residue on live prose) connects the R453 miss-rate to real authoring. Everything an execution session will follow — premise, prompts, recipes, rubric, runbook S0-S11 — lives in one manifest whose sha256 is pinned in this entry before any execution session exists.
changes_bullets: protocol manifest authored and pinned: claudedocs/phase1-ab-experiment/ab-manifest.json, sha256 fd18e42593c128fdd9a128edc439ad70544790e1c9fc2a2c2c9983b1124d1086 — premise ("Saltglass", original, 1923 lighthouse mystery; extraction corpora excluded to avoid familiarity contamination), structure quota (>=24 scenes / 2 forks / >=3 world-lines / >=2 frames / 6 required setups), both agent prompts verbatim, 3-session-per-arm budget with fresh context per session, extraction recipe, <=8-rule derivation recipe (ratified classes only), defect metrics D1-D4, 5-axis judge rubric, predictions, validity conditions, session runbook S0-S11

fairness keystone: the graded artifact is NEVER the loop agent's own store — one blind extractor re-extracts BOTH proses (labels shuffled story-A/B) under one pinned recipe (discovery verbs R458/R459 + R462/R463, import manifests from explicit text only, canon order from each story's own scene graph interleaved at forks per R445 F5); symmetric by construction, closes the graded-by-its-own-referee objection

defect endpoint = D1 gated rule violations + D2 unchained_state_pairs + D3 dangling among the 6 REQUIRED setups + D4 fork-boundary faults; craft surfaces (payoffs_to_unmarked / cross_scope / undecidable) reported never counted — dashboards never gate, even inside an experiment; loop-arm in-session gate catches logged as PREVENTED defects, an economics row never mixed into the endpoint

preference leg rides R466: per-world linear reading copies (manuscript verb = order/membership authority for store-backed copies — this experiment is the verb's pulling consumer), world-lines matched by fork-choice correspondence, 3 fresh LLM judges + optional owner read; bridge finding defined: judge-cited continuity errors unmatched by any D-metric = the V7/V8 untyped residue measured on live authored prose

Round 470 — session review of R467-R469:.decision_summary: Textbook review of the session's three rounds under the standing criteria (no hacks, long-term structure, North Star, SSOT). Verdict: doctrine intact, but five findings — all in the session's own work, all of the honesty-surface or symmetry class it claims to uphold — and all are repaid here rather than carried. The instructive pair: F2 found ambiguity inside the just-pinned A/B manifest (which store backs the loop story's reading copy — an orchestrator degree of freedom is a hole in a BLIND protocol), fixed by making the blind re-extracted store the single authority for ordering BOTH arms' judge copies and re-pinning the manifest pre-execution, the pin chain itself now the tamper evidence; and F3 found this session extending the very parse-then-ignore class R466 had rejected (query-mode flags silently outranking each other), fixed by loud mutual exclusion in one parse site with unit tests. F1 closed a test-coverage overclaim by adding the missing direction of the roster<->scanner equivalence pin; F4 applied no-silent-caps to list_changelog's own motivating consumer (216 full entries into a session-load context) via a limit that never hides the true total; F5 tightened the ordering contract prose. Pattern note for future reviews: every finding came from asking whether the session's artifacts obey the rules the session itself cited.
changes_bullets: F1 (R468 test overclaim): the roster<->scanner comment claimed both drift directions fail CI but only roster-name->hit was tested; query_term_every_scanned_field_is_in_its_roster added (unfiltered sweep of the fully-populated store, every emitted field_path base name must be in its kind's roster), fixture extracted to one fully_populated_store helper + ROSTER_CASES const shared by the direction pair, comment now names both tests

F2 (R469 protocol ambiguity): the pinned manifest's reading-copy rule was asymmetric and ambiguous about which store backs the loop story's copy — an orchestrator degree of freedom inside a blind protocol; amended: BOTH arms' judge copies are ordered by report-playthrough-manuscript over the blind RE-EXTRACTED stores (the defect referee also orders the reading; R466 honesty surfaces logged for both arms), prose injected by scene id; pin chain = R469 fd18e425...1086 superseded by 30ed5ed6b385cb4beb0b7e5b481179bd1e6162d831fe334ec8597609406a1f7e, both pre-execution, sec 7.18 + sec 10 synced

F3 (R467 extended a parse-then-ignore chain): query modes had silent dispatch precedence (--list-sections outranking --list-changelog etc.) — the exact class the R466 --world guard rejects; parse_query_args now rejects >1 mode loudly and mode-scoped flags without their mode (--limit w/o --list-changelog, --regex/--scope/--field w/o --term, --include-* w/o a section id); 3 parse unit tests

F4 (R467 unbounded payload vs its own consumer): list_changelog returned all entries unconditionally — the motivating session-load consumer pulls 216 full views into context; limit threaded through the single query-crate home (in-place signature change, all callers updated same change, no postfix versioning): ChangelogLedgerView {total, entries} with limit keeping the newest n and total always full (no-silent-caps), CLI --limit N + honest "showing newest n of M" stderr, MCP limit arg, shape change from bare array declared here (the R460 rule: the deviating round records the deviation)
F5 (doc accuracy): query_term ordering contract now states identifier-key-first scan order

CLI live: --list-changelog --limit 3 prints exactly R467/R468/R469 + "showing newest 3 of 216"; --limit without the mode, --list-sections --list-changelog, and --term x 39 all reject loudly naming the conflict; MCP stdio live: list_changelog limit=2 returns total=216 entries=2 newest-last

Round 471 — A/B authoring experiment exe.decision_summary: Executed the R469/R470-pinned blind A/B authoring experiment end to end (S1-S11, one day, claude-fable-5 throughout): plain vs Mnemosyne-loop agents each authored the Saltglass branching novella in 3 fresh sessions, one blind session re-extracted both proses under one recipe, three blind judges read matched world-line copies, and the reveal round graded the sealed predictions. Headline: the only pinned outcome (PRED-1 floor) is REFUTED on both conjuncts — the plain story re-extracted to 0 primary-endpoint defects (pin required >=3) and the loop story to 1 (boots-custody fork-boundary fault the loop author never recorded), so loop < plain failed too. The control self-organized: the plain agent invented an unprompted in-file continuity ledger, turning the comparison into freeform-notes vs typed-store+gates — at 29-30 scenes on this model tier, notes suffice for every deterministically-detectable class. What the experiment bought instead: PRED-4 confirmed with margin (judges cited >=4 real continuity defects on the plain story that no D-metric caught, and ~8 across both arms vs 1 gated total — the untyped residue dominates, and fork-seam leaks are its dominant class in BOTH arms), PRED-3 landed at 1.48x tokens/scene (below the predicted 1.5-4x band; overhead is cache-read, friction decays to 0 by session 3), preference PLAIN 2-1/6-3 with the recorded weighting inverted (judges chose whole-world bookkeeping over the loop's admitted prose+knowledge-discipline superiority), and the loop's protection was measured as min(store-coverage, gate-power) with coverage binding. Full tables and analysis: claudedocs/phase1-ab-experiment/ab-report.md (run-log, verdicts, sealed label map alongside).
changes_bullets: S1-S3 plain arm: 29 scenes / 3 worlds / 3 endings authored bare in /home/coin/saltglass-novella across 3 fresh sessions; the agent self-invented an in-file continuity ledger (ground truth, custody tables, payoff-debt list) — an unprompted paper Mnemosyne, making the control freeform-notes rather than nothing

S4-S6 loop arm: 30 scenes / 3 worlds / 3 endings authored store-first in /home/coin/saltglass-studio (71 facts, 4 predicates+rules, declared canon order, gates green every session); 3 repairs all substrate-friction (coordinate prefix, rules shape, canon-order shape), decaying to 0 by session 3 — no story-class gate catch during authoring

S7: coin-flip shuffle, label map sealed (sha256 in run log), both proses blind-re-extracted under one recipe into fresh stores (A: 145 facts/6 rules, B: 136 facts/7 rules); defect tables: PLAIN 0, LOOP 1 (boots custody fork-boundary, rule_exclusive_overlap — a state the loop author never recorded); 3 matched reading-copy pairs assembled
S8-S10: three blind judge sessions; preference PLAIN 2-1 by judge / 6-3 by pair, confront-reveal unanimous PLAIN; loop scored 5/5 character-knowledge on all lines and lost on whole-world bookkeeping
